title: Architecture Overview
summary: The MVP uses a portable architecture with repo-backed content, static retrieval artifacts, Cloudflare deployment, and OpenAI-backed generation.
body: The system keeps content, retrieval, AI-provider, and platform concerns isolated. That allows the first release to stay inexpensive while leaving a clear migration path to Postgres, pgvector, or alternate hosting.

title: Agent Delivery Model
summary: The project is delivered through specialized agent roles with explicit handoffs, artifacts, and acceptance criteria.
body: Each agent owns a narrow slice of the system and hands off decisions in a structured format. The output is a repository that doubles as an implementation artifact and a public case study.

title: Content Model
summary: The source of truth lives in repo-managed structured files for resume roles, projects, explainers, FAQ, and build documents.
body: This keeps the MVP simple, reviewable, and cheap to operate. The retrieval layer treats all of these materials as evidence sources while preserving stable IDs for later migration into a database.
